questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A sector representing $25\%$ of a total is shown shaded on the pie chart. Compute its central angle (degrees).

figure.caption: Pie sector
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 145 80 Z' fill='#2563eb'/><text x='30' y='158' font-size='8'>shaded = 25% of pie</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Central angle $=\text{fraction}\times360^\circ$.

**Working.**
$$\theta=0.25\times360=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of $10, 20, 30, 40$ and $50$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum divided by count.

**Working.**
$$\frac{10+20+30+40+50}{5}=\frac{150}{5}=30$$

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows four values: $10, 20, 30, 40$. Compute their total.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='100' width='25' height='20'/><rect x='85' y='80' width='25' height='40'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='40' width='25' height='80'/></g><text x='50' y='138' font-size='8'>10, 20, 30, 40</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the values.

**Working.**
$$10+20+30+40=100$$

**Answer.** $100$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In the right triangle shown, the legs are $3$ and $4$. The length of the hypotenuse is _____ .

figure.caption: Right triangle with legs 3 and 4 and the right angle at the bottom-left.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='40,130 160,130 40,40' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='40' y='118' width='12' height='12' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1'/><text x='95' y='145' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>4</text><text x='20' y='90' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>3</text><text x='105' y='80' font-size='12' fill='#ef4444'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pythagoras: hypotenuse $=\sqrt{a^2+b^2}$.

**Working.** $\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=\sqrt{25}=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $600$ is divided in the ratio $2:3$. The larger part is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share equals the total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** Larger fraction $=\dfrac{3}{2+3}=\dfrac{3}{5}$, so
$$600\times\frac{3}{5}=360$$

**Answer.** $360$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a code, 'ROCK' is written as 'QNBJ'. Using the same rule, 'SAND' is written as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Determine the shift: R→Q is $-1$.

**Working.** Each letter shifts back by one: S→R, A→Z, N→M, D→C gives RZMC.

**Answer.** RZMC.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'Critics argue that subsidies for fossil-fuel extraction distort energy markets and delay the transition to renewables. Proponents counter that such subsidies protect jobs and keep energy affordable in the short term.'

The passage primarily presents:
options: two opposing viewpoints on fossil-fuel subsidies
a definitive argument against renewable energy
a neutral historical account of energy policy
evidence proving subsidies have no effect
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Identify the rhetorical structure (argument, narrative, comparison of views).

**Working.** The passage states what 'critics argue' and what 'proponents counter', i.e., two opposing positions, without endorsing either.

**Answer.** two opposing viewpoints on fossil-fuel subsidies.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Quantitative Aptitude — CI vs SI Difference
questions.stem: The difference between the compound interest and the simple interest on $10000$ at $10\%$ per annum for $2$ years is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For $2$ years the difference is $P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$.

**Working.**
$$10000\times (0.1)^2 = 10000\times 0.01 = 100$$

**Answer.** $100$.

questions.stem: Triangle is to $3$ sides as the pentagon shown is to

figure.caption: A regular pentagon.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The analogy maps a polygon to its number of sides.

**Working.** A pentagon has $5$ sides.

**Answer.** 5 sides.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The volume of a cube of side $4$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Volume of a cube $=\text{side}^3$.

**Working.** $4^3=64$.

**Answer.** $64$.

**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{5\times1000}{500}=10.$$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $12\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 28</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$12\times28=336.$$

**Answer.** $336$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $3$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 180" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><polygon points="40,150 280,150 240,118 80,118" fill="#16a34a" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="80,118 240,118 205,86 115,86" fill="#65a30d" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="115,86 205,86 178,54 142,54" fill="#d97706" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="142,54 178,54 165,30 155,30" fill="#dc2626" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="160" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Producers = 10000</text><text x="160" y="110" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L2 = ?</text><text x="160" y="78" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L3 = ?</text><text x="160" y="46" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L4 = ?</text><text x="160" y="172" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kcal/m²/yr — 10% transfer per level</text></svg>
figure.caption: Only the producer (trophic level 1) energy is marked; upper levels follow the 10% rule. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$10000\times0.1^{2}=100.$$

**Answer.** $100$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 400 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2.4 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $v_s=\text{depth}\div\text{time}$, both read from the trajectory.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2.4\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=6\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $6\,\text{mm/s}$

**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

questions.stem: Wet MSW $1500\,\text{kg}$ at $40\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1500(1-40/100)=900.$$

**Answer.** $900\,\text{kg}$

questions.stem: A municipality proposes to mine its sole confined aquifer to exhaustion within 15 years to bankroll current expansion, leaving the next generation with no water source. This plan MOST directly violates which pillar of sustainable development?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Exhausting a resource so future generations cannot meet their own needs breaches inter-generational equity — the core of the Brundtland definition.

**Working.**
Map the scenario to the violated principle.

**Answer.** Inter-generational equity

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: An exporter's overseas buyers demand third-party certification of an audited, continually-improving system that reduces the environmental impact of its operations — not product quality, not worker safety, not information security. The exporter should certify to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Match the scope to the standard: ISO 14001 = Environmental Management System (PDCA over environmental aspects); ISO 9001 = quality; ISO 45001 = occupational health & safety; ISO 27001 = information security.

**Working.**
Scope is environmental impact ⇒ ISO 14001.

**Answer.** ISO 14001

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1500\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1500(1-60/100)=600.$$

**Answer.** $600\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: The nutrient most commonly limiting and therefore triggering eutrophication in freshwater lakes is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Phosphorus is usually the limiting nutrient in freshwater; its loading drives algal blooms.

**Working.**
Phosphorus.

**Answer.** Phosphorus

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A treatment plant receives BOD $250\,\text{mg/L}$ and discharges $25\,\text{mg/L}$. The BOD removal efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 250</text><rect x="190" y="138" width="46" height="12" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="134" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 25</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">BOD (mg/L)</text></svg>
figure.caption: BOD: influent vs. effluent (mg/L).

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\eta=\dfrac{S_0-S_e}{S_0}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{250-25}{250}\times100=90\%.$$

**Answer.** $90\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: A tannery's discharge requires $₹4.5$ lakh of effluent-treatment (abatement) and additionally causes $₹2.8$ lakh of measurable downstream crop damage. Under the polluter-pays principle, the amount (in $₹$ lakh) the tannery must internalise is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The polluter-pays principle internalises the full externality: the polluter bears abatement **plus** damage costs (not just one of them).

**Working.**
$$4.5+2.8=7.3\text{ lakh}.$$

**Answer.** $₹7.3$ lakh

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Compared with a slow sand filter, a rapid sand filter is characterised by
options: higher filtration rate and reliance on prior coagulation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rapid sand filters run at ~100–125 times the slow-filter rate, need coagulation–sedimentation upstream, and are cleaned by backwashing.

**Working.**
Higher rate, needs coagulation.

**Answer.** High rate, coagulation-dependent

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Alkalinity of natural water is primarily a measure of its capacity to

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="18" x2="38" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="160" x2="305" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="14" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="240" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant added →</text><line x1="38" y1="89" x2="305" y2="89" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 3"/><text x="42" y="86" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">pH 7</text><path d="M38 150 C120 146 160 140 168 89 C176 38 230 32 305 28" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="168" cy="89" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence pt</text></svg>
figure.caption: Titration curve: sharp pH jump at the equivalence point.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Alkalinity = acid-neutralising capacity (mainly HCO₃⁻/CO₃²⁻/OH⁻).

**Working.**
Acid-neutralising capacity.

**Answer.** Neutralise acid

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=40\%$, $H=7\%$, $O=35\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 17.2</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(40)+1419(7-35/8)=17205.$$

**Answer.** $17205\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹50,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 6</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{50000}{(1+0.08)^{6}}=31508.$$

**Answer.** $₹31508$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.02\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $15$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 2000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$2000e^{0.02\times15}=2700.$$

**Answer.** $2700$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $8\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$ of flue gas. Reading the collecting area $A$ and the drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its Deutsch–Anderson collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 300 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Deutsch–Anderson: $\eta=1-e^{-wA/Q}$, with $A$ and $w$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-e^{-0.12\times300/8}\right)\times100=98.89\%.$$

**Answer.** $98.89\%$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues

figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

$$80+2\times28+2\times265=666.$$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=150\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.5</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 60</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.5\times150}{60+150}=0.357.$$

**Answer.** $0.357$
